title: campanula rotundifolia f. hirta
intro: Campanula rotundifolia f. hirta, also known as hairy bellflower, is a flowering plant species in the genus Campanula, native to Northern Europe and Asia. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The hairy bellflower is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are taken to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health concerns.
